sourceMovie.title: Abduction
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Abduction for the same reason it broke through in 2011: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a action rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. John Singleton keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The thriller and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Salt
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Abduction clicked for you, Salt (2010) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and action, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Abduction?
faq.answer: Try Salt, Eagle Eye, Conspiracy Theory—each shares DNA with Abduction while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Abduction?
faq.answer: Abduction (2011) is best described as Thriller, Action, Mystery. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Abduction worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 5.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this thriller lane.
